quantity: Osterwalder-Schrader reconstruction obligation for phi^4 in 1+1D
observable: Schwinger functions S_n(x_1,...,x_n) for the mass-tuned finite-volume phi^4 theory
purpose: Connect the finite-volume Schwinger two-point function to Wightman axioms (OS reconstruction). This is required to claim that the simulation produces a quantum field, not just a family of finite matrices.

foundation.schwinger_two_point: The Schwinger two-point function G(x;K,N) = <phi(x)phi(0)> is the observable tracked by the interaction delta chain.
foundation.finite_volume_constraint: Only finite spatial volume L is permitted as a final constraint — not K, N, or any other regulator.
foundation.post_k8_result: The post-K8 K-tail bound and (when proved) the N-limit theorem establish that G(x;K,N) → G(x) as K,N→∞ for fixed volume L.
foundation.what_remains: OS reconstruction asks: does the family of Schwinger functions (at all n-point orders) satisfy the OS axioms (reflection positivity, covariance, clustering) in the limit?

os_axioms_required.description: S_n(gx_1,...,gx_n) = S_n(x_1,...,x_n) for g in the Euclidean group. In finite volume: translational covariance within the volume.
For test functions f supported in the forward time half-space, sum_{i,j} S_2(x_i - x_j') f_i^* f_j ≥ 0. This is the key axiom connecting Euclidean and Minkowski theories.
S_n is symmetric (Bose statistics for scalar phi^4).
S_n factorizes in the infinite-separation limit: S_2(x-y) → S_1(x)S_1(y) as |x-y|→∞.

proof_target.description: For the mass-tuned phi^4 theory in 1+1D finite volume, prove that the limiting Schwinger functions (after K,N→∞) satisfy the OS axioms, and apply the OS reconstruction theorem to produce a Wightman QFT.
key_lemmas_needed: reflection positivity of the finite-volume lattice approximant (or Hamiltonian truncation approximant)
stability of reflection positivity under K,N→∞ limits
clustering property in the infinite-volume or large-volume limit
applicability of OS reconstruction theorem (Osterwalder-Schrader 1973, 1975) to this class of Schwinger functions
known_results: OS reconstruction is proved for phi^4 in 2D (Glimm-Jaffe 1973, GRS 1975)
The Hamiltonian truncation approach preserves Hermiticity (verified in evidence chain)
Reflection positivity for Euclidean lattice phi^4 follows from Osterwalder-Seiler
The finite-volume Schwinger function for this theory is computed by the candidate
proof_target.gap: The finite-volume truncation method (Fock-space Hamiltonian) differs from Euclidean path integral — reflection positivity for the Hamiltonian truncation approach needs direct verification.

forbidden_shortcuts: claim OS reconstruction from finite K/N evidence alone
assert Wightman axioms without proving OS reconstruction theorem applies
use the Euclidean lattice RP proof directly without verifying it applies to Hamiltonian truncation
skip clustering verification for the finite-volume theory
no_workaround_status: not achieved; this obligation defines the OS reconstruction requirement. Until proved, the candidate computes finite matrix approximants, not a quantum field.
